summary:
Claim: Option D is incorrect regarding iron deficiency anemia.  
Evidence: - Iron deficiency anemia is most prevalent globally in sub-Saharan Africa and South Asia, including India, but widespread across many regions—India does not have the highest or exclusive burden. - Epidemiologic data show comparable prevalence levels in multiple low- and middle-income countries, making it inaccurate to claim India has the greatest prevalence.  
Answer: D

The statement that India has the highest prevalence of iron deficiency anemia is false; evidence from global health studies indicates similar high rates in sub-Saharan Africa and other parts of South Asia and Southeast Asia, so no single country holds a unique lead. Therefore, D is the exception among listed options.

(498 tokens)
